title: crataegus × grandiflora
intro: Crataegus × grandiflora is a hybrid hawthorn plant, a cross between Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus laevigata, known for its dense, thorny branches and showy white flowers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for centuries due to its rich content of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular protective properties. The medicinal properties of Crataegus × grandiflora have been associated with various health benefits, including reduced blood pressure, improved cardiovascular function, and enhanced antioxidant status, which can contribute to a decreased risk of heart disease and other chronic conditions. Crataegus × grandiflora can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and overall well-being.
